translation_text.an3.140:1.1: ‚ÄúMendicants, I will teach you about three wild colts and three wild people.
translation_text.an3.140:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.‚Äù
translation_text.an3.140:2.2: One wild colt is fast, but not beautiful or well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:2.3: Another wild colt is fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:2.4: While another wild colt is fast, beautiful, and well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:2.5: These are the three wild colts.
translation_text.an3.140:3.1: And what are the three wild people?
translation_text.an3.140:3.2: One wild person is fast, but not beautiful or well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:3.3: Another wild person is fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:3.4: While another wild person is fast, beautiful, and well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:4.1: And how is a wild person fast, but not beautiful or well proportioned?
translation_text.an3.140:4.2: It‚Äôs when a mendicant truly understands: ‚ÄòThis is suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the origin of suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the cessation of suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering‚Äô.
translation_text.an3.140:4.3: This is how they‚Äôre fast, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:4.4: But when asked a question about the teaching or training, they falter without answering.
translation_text.an3.140:4.5: This is how they‚Äôre not beautiful, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:4.6: And they don‚Äôt receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an3.140:4.7: This is how they‚Äôre not well proportioned, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:4.8: This is how a wild person is fast, but not beautiful or well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:5.1: And how is a wild person fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned?
translation_text.an3.140:5.2: It‚Äôs when a mendicant truly understands: ‚ÄòThis is suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the origin of suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the cessation of suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering‚Äô.
translation_text.an3.140:5.3: This is how they‚Äôre fast, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:5.4: When asked a question about the teaching or training, they answer without faltering.
translation_text.an3.140:5.5: This is how they‚Äôre beautiful, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:5.6: But they don‚Äôt receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an3.140:5.7: This is how they‚Äôre not well proportioned, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:5.8: This is how a wild person is fast and beautiful, but not well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:6.1: And how is a wild person fast, beautiful, and well proportioned?
translation_text.an3.140:6.2: It‚Äôs when a mendicant truly understands: ‚ÄòThis is suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the origin of suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the cessation of suffering‚Äô ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄòThis is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering‚Äô.
translation_text.an3.140:6.3: This is how they‚Äôre fast, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:6.4: When asked a question about the teaching or training, they answer without faltering.
translation_text.an3.140:6.5: This is how they‚Äôre beautiful, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:6.6: They receive robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an3.140:6.7: This is how they‚Äôre well proportioned, I say.
translation_text.an3.140:6.8: This is how a wild person is fast, beautiful, and well proportioned.
translation_text.an3.140:6.9: These are the three wild people.‚Äù
